name: Lambda, map, filter
description: Questions on lambda, map(), and filter().

questions.question: lambda x: x + 1 is:
choices: A function that takes x and returns x+1
questions.explanation: lambda creates an anonymous function; here it adds 1 to its argument.

questions.question: list(map(lambda x: x*2, [1,2,3])) returns:
questions.explanation: map applies the function to each element; list() collects the results.

questions.question: list(filter(lambda x: x > 0, [-1,0,1,2])) returns:
questions.explanation: filter keeps only elements for which the function is true; 1 and 2 are > 0.

questions.question: A lambda in Python can contain:
questions.explanation: Lambda must be a single expression; no statements, return, or assignments.

questions.question: map and filter in Python 3 return:
questions.explanation: In Python 3, map and filter return iterator objects; wrap in list() to get a list.
